rulings.Case Title: ARTSDECO-ARKANSAS STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. MCKENZIE, ROSE,& HOLLIDAY DEVELOPMENT INC A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 11, 2009, line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT THE LAWSON ROOFING COMPANY, INC., COY SANDERS CONSTRUCTION, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion denied. Cross-defendant does not have standing to bring this motion as to plaintiff's claims. Cross-defendant has not established that it is a real party in interest to the underlying action between plaintiff and defendant. As to the merits of the motion, Motion denied. Cross-defendant failed to shift the burden - that the action is time-barred. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD C KIRCHNER et al VS. DESIGN DEVELOPMENT GROUP PACIFIC VI, LLC A et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 11, 2009, line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT WESTERN WALL SYSTEMS, INC.'S Demurrer To CCP 474 Amendment To Cross-Complaint Of City Building, Inc. rulings.Case Title: DONALD GREENE et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 11, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MICHAEL MCSHANE, PAUL ISAKSON'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Motion granted without leave to amend. Plaintiffs failed to make a tort claim within the requisite six month period. (Govt. Code section 900, et seq.) and failed to apply for late claim relief within the requisite one year period (Govt. Code section 911.4)=(302/CWW/DC)

rulings.Case Title: PARISA MOSAVIAN DDS VS. MASSACHUSETTS MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 11, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT MASSACHUSETTS MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled, in part, and sustained, in part. Demurrer overruled as to the first and second causes of action. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the third cause of action. Plaintiff to plead the fraudulent misrepresentation with greater particularity. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: GARY KING VS. RICHARD MANFREDONIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Prefiling Order For Vexatios Litigant
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 11, 2009, line 14, DEFENDANT RICHARD MANFREDONIA'S Motion For Prefiling Order For Vexatios Litigant. Motion Denied. Insufficient evidence presented that plaintiff qualifies as "vexatious" under CCP section 391. See Holcomb vs. United States Bank Nat'l Ass'n., 129 CA 4th, 1494, and Morton vs. Wagner, 156 CA 4th 963. =(302/CWW/X-A)
